abstract: The performance of machine learning models is often compromised by the presence of redundant and irrelevant features within large datasets, which can lead to significant degradation in predictive accuracy. To address this challenge, this paper introduces IGRF-RFE, a hybrid feature selection framework designed specifically for multi-class network anomaly detection using a multilayer perceptron (MLP) network. By integrating the computational efficiency of filter-based methods with the precision of wrapper-based selection, IGRF-RFE effectively streamlines high-dimensional data while maintaining high classification performance.
